  Thanks for your contribution! Please give this PR a Conventional Commits
  style title, e.g. "feat(api): add bulk signal endpoint".
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do and why? Explain the motivation, not just the code. -->

## Related issue

<!-- Link the issue this PR addresses, e.g. "Closes #123". -->

## Type of change

- [ ] `fix` — bug fix (non-breaking)
- [ ] `feat` — new feature (non-breaking)
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` / `perf` / `style` — no behaviour change
- [ ] `test` / `ci` / `build` / `chore` — tooling
- [ ] **BREAKING CHANGE** — changes a public API contract

## Checklist

- [ ] PR title follows [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
- [ ] `ruff check src/` passes
- [ ] Tests added/updated and passing locally (`pytest`)
- [ ] Docs updated if behaviour or configuration changed
- [ ] No secrets, `.env`, or generated artefacts committed
- [ ] For breaking changes: `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer added and version
      bumped in `pyproject.toml`
